title: Extended machine-readable Wildside user experience state graph
description: A route-aware, local-first, backend-compatible UX state graph for leynos/wildside-mockup-v2a, intended to pair with leynos/wildside and leynos/wildside-engine.

included: User-facing routes and transitions in the mockup sitemap
Local-first/offline persistence states from Wildside PWA docs
Asynchronous backend route-generation states from backend architecture and engine docs
Reasonable extrapolations needed to turn fixtures/no-ops into backend-compatible production behaviour
excluded: Backend ETL details for OSM/Wikidata ingestion
Exact PostGIS schemas and tile serving internals
Native wrapper concerns for Capacitor/Tauri beyond PWA route state
Payment/subscription flows, not present in the current mockup/API docs

stateTypes.route: A URL-addressable user-facing page or route leaf.
stateTypes.route_layout: Route wrapper that scopes providers or persistent UI, usually not a standalone page.
stateTypes.route_substate: URL hash or route-internal substate that is addressable or recoverable.
stateTypes.substate: Non-addressable view state inside a route.
stateTypes.overlay: Dialog, toast, permission prompt, or panel over an underlying state.
stateTypes.transient: Short-lived internal state used for redirect, delay, or optimistic transition.
stateTypes.async: Waiting/progress state for a network or background process.
stateTypes.parallel: Orthogonal runtime region that can coexist with the route graph.
stateTypes.route_future: Future URL-addressable page implied by architecture but absent from the current mockup.

basis.documented: Declared in sitemap, design docs, API docs, or architecture docs.
basis.extrapolated: Plausible production behaviour needed to connect documented architecture to the mocked UX.
basis.future: Supported by architecture/contracts, but intentionally staged after the current mockup surface.

mockup_repo.role: Concrete routed PWA mockup and UI implementation.

mockup_sitemap.role: Canonical mockup route structure, primary navigation, and documented route-to-route transitions.

mockup_data_model.role: Mockup-derived backend-compatible data layers, route-generation endpoint plan, user-state model, and offline bundle plan.

mockup_card_architecture.role: Card inventory and data-driven localized rendering principle.

mockup_app_root.role: App-level providers for units, display mode, theme, and router.

mockup_welcome.role: Welcome CTA to Discover and value proposition content.

mockup_discover.role: Initial interest selection, skip/start actions, and mock generation delay before Explore.

mockup_explore.role: Explore catalogue, search, categories, featured routes, theme grids, collections, trending routes, community pick, and bottom navigation.

mockup_customize.role: Custom route controls, preview selection, bottom navigation, advanced toggles, and currently disabled regenerate/start buttons.

mockup_quick_walk.role: Quick walk map, duration/interests controls, hash tabs, save action, and wizard transition.

mockup_map_state.role: Shared MapLibre viewport, POI highlight, layer visibility, and locale actions.

mockup_map_bare.role: Bare map screen and tab/fab navigation to quick walk/saved.

mockup_itinerary.role: Itinerary map, tabs, route actions, disabled locate, POI overlay panels, share and favourite toggles.

mockup_saved.role: Saved route detail, map/stops/notes tabs, favourite/share/customize/offline/start actions, and empty state.

mockup_wizard.role: Three-step duration/interests, discovery/accessibility, and review/confirm wizard with saved dialog.

mockup_offline.role: Offline storage overview, suggestions, auto-management toggles, download list, manage/delete/undo behaviour, and add-area dialog.

mockup_safety.role: Safety/accessibility accordion, toggle state, preset alert, save preferences dialog, and back to Explore.

mockup_walk_complete.role: Completion summary, rate toast, share dialog, save action, and remix-to-wizard action.

wildside_readme.role: Product concept: mobile serendipity engine generating bespoke walking tours from OSM/Wikidata and optimizing for interestingness.

wildside_pwa_design.role: Local-first PWA runtime behaviour, state boundaries, map architecture, REST/WS split, offline-first persistence, and staged migration.

wildside_pwa_data_model.role: Backend-compatible PWA data model, generated route plan, user state, offline bundles, outbox, endpoint set, and persistence strategy.

wildside_backend_architecture.role: Hexagonal modular monolith, REST endpoints, WebSocket status updates, route jobs, idempotency, trace IDs, and backend observability.

wildside_asyncapi.role: WebSocket contract shape for onboarding events and trace/idempotency conventions.

engine_readme.role: Engine purpose: combine OSM geospatial data, Wikidata semantics, and orienteering solver for personalized walking tours.

engine_users_guide.role: Engine SolveRequest/SolveResponse, POI store, scoring, solver contract, and integration flow.

semantics: converted to HTTP request at drain time

catalogueExplore.cachePolicy: network-first with cached fallback; show stale-but-available copy when offline

interestThemes.cachePolicy: available offline as slow-changing descriptors/catalogue entities

userPreferences.writePolicy: optimistic where safe; include expectedRevision once preferences exist; queue offline writes in Dexie outbox

routeGeneration.writePolicy: use Idempotency-Key; backend enqueues job; client polls or listens for WS progress

routeAnnotations.writePolicy: client-generated note/session/progress IDs where applicable; offline mutations queued; idempotent writes

offlineBundles.writePolicy: bundle manifest is durable state; tile bytes live in Cache Storage or Dexie; deletes evict metadata and bytes

walkSessions.writePolicy: create client UUID at navigation start; persist completion projection at walk end or queue offline

states.title: App bootstrap and provider restore
states.description: Restore app shell providers, locale/unit/display/theme state, persisted query cache, service worker registration, and router before the first screen.

states.title: Root redirect
states.description: Canonical entry route redirects immediately to /welcome.

states.title: Unknown route fallback
states.description: Client route not found or unsupported deep link. Offer safe return to Explore or Map.

states.title: Network online
states.description: Network is available. Query revalidation and outbox drain may run.

states.title: Network offline
states.description: Network is unavailable. Render cached data, queue mutations, and show stale/offline affordances where relevant.

states.title: Outbox idle
states.description: No pending offline mutations.

states.title: Outbox queued
states.description: One or more mutations are queued in Dexie because the network is offline or the mutation is deferred.

states.title: Outbox draining
states.description: Queued mutations are being sent with stable idempotency keys.

states.title: Sync conflict
states.description: Server rejected an optimistic write or idempotency replay due to revision or payload conflict.

states.title: Service worker update available
states.description: A new PWA version is staged and may be activated through an explicit user-visible update flow.

states.title: Welcome
states.description: First product value-proposition screen with CTA into Discover.

states.title: Discover interest selection
states.description: New user chooses interest themes; default selections are pre-populated and can be changed with a multi-select toggle group.

states.title: Discover mock generation
states.description: Prototype-only generation delay after Start; production should persist preferences or queue them before moving to Explore.

states.title: Explore catalogue
states.description: Catalogue home with route search, categories, featured route, themes, collections, trending routes, community pick, and bottom navigation.
persistence: TanStack Query cache persisted to IndexedDB

states.title: Explore search text active
states.description: Search text has been entered. Results are filtered locally from cached catalogue unless a future remote search endpoint is added.

states.title: Explore category filter active
states.description: A category/theme filter is selected, narrowing catalogue projections while keeping cached catalogue as the source of truth.

states.title: Explore stale but available
states.description: Catalogue renders from persisted cache after network-first fetch fails. User should see a stale/offline hint, not a blank screen.

states.title: Explore catalogue unavailable
states.description: No cached catalogue exists and fetch fails. Provide recovery to retry, use Quick Walk with defaults, or open Offline manager.

states.title: Customize route controls
states.description: Route customizer with distance/duration sliders, crowd/elevation segments, surface picker, interest mix, previews, and advanced options.

states.title: Customize preview selected
states.description: A preview route card is selected. Current mockup updates selected preview but keeps Start/Regenerate disabled.

states.title: Customize generation requested
states.description: Planned production state for enabled Regenerate/Start buttons. Builds a route-generation request from customizer controls.
persistence: Dexie outbox if offline is allowed for deferred generation

states.title: Wizard step 1: duration and interests
states.description: Set walk duration and active interests. Back returns to Explore; Help is a placeholder alert; Next advances to Step 2.

states.title: Wizard step 2: discovery and accessibility
states.description: Adjust hidden-gems versus hotspots and toggle accessibility/safety options. Back returns to Step 1; Next advances to Step 3.

states.title: Wizard step 3: review generated walk
states.description: Review summary, preferences applied, featured stops, and weather copy. Back returns to Step 2; Reset returns to Step 1; Go opens a saved-walk confirmation dialog.

states.title: Wizard saved-walk dialog
states.description: Dialog shown after Go. Current mockup says the walk is saved and offers Close or View on map; its View on map action navigates to Saved.
persistence: SavedRoute mutation or local fixture in production

states.title: Route generation draft
states.description: User inputs have been assembled into a SolveRequest-compatible draft: start location, time budget, interest profile, safety/accessibility constraints, route seed, and optional maximum nodes.

states.title: Route generation HTTP request
states.description: POST /api/v1/routes has been issued with an idempotency key and trace context. Backend responds with request_id when queued.

states.title: Route generation queued
states.description: Backend has accepted the request and queued GenerateRouteJob. Client can poll or listen for WebSocket progress.

states.title: Route generation progress
states.description: Route job is running. Progress may arrive via WebSocket route_generation_status events or polling.

states.title: Route generation succeeded
states.description: A RoutePlan is available with geometry, ordered stops, and inline POIs for offline-friendly map rendering.

states.title: Route generation failed
states.description: Route generation failed. Show reason, trace ID if available, and recovery actions.

states.title: Route generation idempotency conflict
states.description: The same idempotency key was reused with a different payload. Regenerate key or replay matching request.

states.title: Route generation data sparse
states.description: Too few candidate POIs were found. Backend may enqueue enrichment; UX can offer broader radius, longer duration, or a curated route.

states.title: Route generation abandoned by user
states.description: User leaves or dismisses generation UI while backend may continue. Keep request recoverable by request_id and do not orphan cache updates.
persistence: Persist active route request until terminal status or TTL

states.title: Map layout with provider
states.description: Map route layout wraps map-related screens in a shared MapStateProvider so viewport, highlights, layers, and locale survive overlay changes.

states.title: Bare map
states.description: Map screen with toolbar, bottom tab-like controls into Quick Walk hash tabs, and saved-route FAB. Used when quick-walk panels are dismissed.

states.title: Quick Walk: map tab
states.description: Interactive quick-walk generator with map, duration slider, interest toggles, and active Explore tab.

states.title: Quick Walk: stops tab
states.description: Stops tab displays the route-flow list for quick-walk POIs and highlights hovered/focused POIs on the shared map.

states.title: Quick Walk: notes tab
states.description: Notes tab shows planning notes for the quick walk.

states.title: Location permission prompt
states.description: Prompt user for location only when needed for current-location route start or locate button. Permission result is stored separately from route state.

states.title: Location unavailable or denied
states.description: GPS or location permission is unavailable. Allow manual start point, last known location, or map centre as route start.

states.title: Map canvas unavailable
states.description: MapLibre failed to initialise or tile style/source failed. Keep overlays usable and expose retry/offline options.

states.title: Itinerary: map tab
states.description: Route itinerary view with map, top stats, route action buttons, and map/stops/notes tabs.

states.title: Itinerary: stops tab
states.description: Stops overlay displays ordered POIs; dismiss returns to map tab. Hover/focus highlights POIs on the shared map.

states.title: Itinerary: notes tab
states.description: Notes overlay displays route notes, route metrics, and future note-authoring affordances.

states.title: Itinerary share dialog
states.description: Share dialog shows preview link and a coming-soon secondary action.

states.title: In-walk navigation active
states.description: Production walking session while following a route. Progress updates visited stop IDs, upcoming POI highlights, and optional turn-by-turn instructions.

states.title: In-walk navigation paused
states.description: User paused navigation or tracking lost confidence. Preserve route and progress; resume when safe.

states.title: Saved routes empty
states.description: No saved routes are available. Current mockup has a guard for this but fixture data normally prevents it.

states.title: Saved route: map tab
states.description: Saved route detail with map, route showcase panel, favourite toggle, view details, customize, offline, share, and start route actions.

states.title: Saved route: stops tab
states.description: Stops list for a saved route. Dismiss returns to map tab; hover/focus highlights map POIs.

states.title: Saved route: notes tab
states.description: Notes/route details tab with metrics, tags, description, and notes.

states.title: Saved route share dialog
states.description: Dialog shows route share URL and notes sharing is not wired up yet.

states.title: Saved favourite toggled
states.description: Optimistically toggles favourite/saved status. Production should write a route annotation/favourite mutation.

states.title: Walk completion summary
states.description: Post-walk completion screen with stats, favourite moments, rating, sharing, saving, and remixing.

states.title: Walk completion rating toast
states.description: Toast confirms rating saved after rate action.

states.title: Walk completion share dialog
states.description: Dialog for choosing/generated share card channels. Real sharing integration is future work.

states.title: Offline manager
states.description: Offline download management screen with storage overview, suggested downloads, existing download cards, auto-management toggles, add-area dialog, and profile nav tab active.

states.title: Offline add-area dialog
states.description: Dialog opened by Add area action. Production should collect route/region bounds and queue bundle creation.

states.title: Offline manage mode
states.description: Manage mode allows deleting existing download cards or undoing a pending delete. Leaving manage mode commits deletion of entries marked undo in the mockup.

states.title: Offline delete pending undo
states.description: A download card has been marked for deletion and can be restored while manage mode is active.
persistence: Deletion not committed until manage mode exits in mockup

states.title: Offline bundle queued
states.description: Bundle manifest has been created locally and/or remotely with status queued.

states.title: Offline bundle downloading
states.description: Bundle tiles/assets are being stored and progress is visible.

states.title: Offline bundle complete
states.description: Bundle is ready for offline use; map tiles and route/catalogue data can be used from local storage.

states.title: Offline bundle failed
states.description: Bundle failed due to network, quota, tile errors, or server errors. Preserve manifest and allow retry/delete.

states.title: Offline storage pressure
states.description: Available device storage is low. Suggest deleting older bundles or enabling auto-management.

states.title: Safety and accessibility preferences
states.description: Safety/accessibility screen with accordion sections, toggles, preset cards, save button, and back to Explore.

states.title: Safety toggle changed
states.description: A safety/accessibility toggle differs from persisted preferences. Save should persist or queue the update.

states.title: Safety preset selected
states.description: Current mockup shows an alert when selecting a preset. Production should apply the preset toggle set, mark preferences dirty, and announce the change non-modally.
uiSurfaces: Preset alert or future non-modal confirmation

states.title: Safety preferences saved dialog
states.description: Dialog shown after Save preferences. Production should display only after mutation is persisted or queued.

states.title: Session unknown
states.description: Initial session state before /users/me or local guest profile resolution. No user-facing route in the current mockup.

states.title: Guest/local profile
states.description: User can browse public catalogue and create local-first state. Writes may queue until login is introduced.

states.title: Authenticated session
states.description: Session cookie is present; user preferences, annotations, routes, and bundles are syncable with backend.

states.title: Login/account flow
states.description: Stage 5 future route for login/account handling. Included only because API contracts already expose login and current-user endpoints.

actions: PUT /api/v1/users/me/interests or /preferences with expectedRevision when available

transitions.label: Next
actions: prepare route summary fixture or request generation in production
subscribe to WS status if connected
surface conflict and regenerate idempotency key
reuse payload with new Idempotency-Key unless safe replay is available

transitions.label: Remix
actions: seed wizard draft from completed route

transitions.label: Apply preset
actions: future: apply preset.appliedToggleIds and mark dirty
clear dirty when write accepted or queued
send queued HTTP mutation with Idempotency-Key
create or load local guest profile

assumptions.title: The graph is backend-compatible rather than prototype-only.
assumptions.detail: States that need backend route generation, persisted preferences, offline outbox, and route progress are included even where the current mockup uses fixtures or no-ops. They are marked extrapolated/future rather than implemented.

assumptions.title: Customize Start/Regenerate are planned states, not current UI actions.
assumptions.detail: The sitemap declares Customize→MapQuick, and the data/API model supports route generation, but the current customizer buttons are disabled. The graph retains disabled no-op transitions and adds planned enabled transitions behind a feature flag.

assumptions.title: Authentication is background/future in the UX graph.
assumptions.detail: The current mockup has no visible auth route. Because the backend exposes login/current-user/preferences contracts and the PWA staging plan lists auth/account handling later, auth is modelled as a parallel runtime region plus a future route.

assumptions.title: Route generation is asynchronous.
assumptions.detail: Production generation uses POST /api/v1/routes, idempotency, a queued background job, polling and/or WebSocket progress. This produces explicit request/queued/progress/succeeded/failed states rather than a single synchronous transition.

assumptions.title: Offline bundles separate manifests from bytes.
assumptions.detail: OfflineBundle manifest state appears in React/server-state caches, but tile bytes remain outside React state in Cache Storage or Dexie. The graph therefore treats bundle lifecycle as parallel persistence state.

assumptions.title: Map overlays must not own the map canvas.
assumptions.detail: Map views share a provider and imperative MapLibre surface; overlay transitions update tabs/highlights/viewport without tearing down the canvas.

assumptions.title: Safety presets should eventually apply state rather than alert.
assumptions.detail: Current preset cards call an alert. The graph records that implemented behaviour, then models a future non-modal state update where the preset applies known toggle IDs and marks preferences dirty.

assertions: selected interests survive until preferences write/queue
poll and WS events patch the same query cache
hash deep links select correct tab
tile bytes are outside React state
queued offline update drains with Idempotency-Key
completion summary avoids fitness-style performance overload
